DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN 'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR'S ANSWERS TO DEFENDANT'S FORM INTERROGATORIES IS GRANTED. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $340, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3 AND 5 BECAUSE THEY ARE NOT BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 BECAUSE ALTHOUGH BASED IN PART ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF RETALIATORY MOTIVE. GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE IT IS BASED AT LEAST IN PART ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A PRIMA FACIE CASE. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR IS ORDERED TO APPEAR FOR HER DEPOSITION, TO BE NOTICED BY DEFENDANT AND TO BE COMPLETED NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2010. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $340, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.
NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT GROSSMAN, JOE HEARING SET FOR FEB-23-2010 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301
DECLARATION OF JAMES M. MILLAR IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS FILED BY PLAINTIFF SAENZPARDO, GERARDO SAENZPARDO, JESUS SAENZPARDO, MARIA VALDAMAR, MERCED

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE DOES NOT IMMUNIZE RETALIATION EVEN THOUGH THE RETALIATION MAY BE EVIDENCED IN PART BY OTHERWISE PRIVILEGED ACTS AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF RETALATION AND OF A SUFFICIENT RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT TO CREATE THE DUTIES ALLEGED IN THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

DISCOVERY 612, DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT ADDITIONAL ORDERS TO RESPOND TO DISCOVERY WOULD NOT RESULT IN COMPLIANCE. PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR'S COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED AND JUDGMENT IS ORDERED ENTERED AGAINST PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST PLAINTIFF MERCED VALDAMAR IN THE AMOUNT OF $500. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-10-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Lisa M. Garcia, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Joe Grossman
No appearances made by Defendant.

RULING: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN's Motion For Terminating Sanctions is granted. The court finds that additional orders to respond to discovery would not result in compliance. Plaintiff Merced Valdamar's complaint is dismissed and judgment is ordered entered against Plaintiff Merced Valdamar. Monetary sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff Merced Valdamar in the amount of $500. Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions IS OFF CALENDAR AND TO BE RENOTICED IN THE DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES M. MILLAR OF MILLAR & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 981-8100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. JEFFREY T. TA OF BLEDSOE LLP, 981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE DOES NOT IMMUNIZE RETALIATION EVEN THOUGH THE RETALIATION MAY BE EVIDENCED IN PART BY OTHERWISE PRIVILEGED ACTS AND PLAINTIFFS HAVE RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF RETALATION AND OF A SUFFICIENT RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT TO CREATE THE DUTIES ALLEGED IN THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. OBJECTIONS WERE OVERRULED. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Defendant'S Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And/ Or Plaintiff'S Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Defendant JOE GROSSMAN 's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Merced Valdamar's Answers To Defendant's Form Interrogatories is granted. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff Merced Valdamar is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answer To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And/ Or Plaintiff'S Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant's Motion To Compel Plaintiff Merced Valdamar's Answers To Defendant's Special Interrogatories is granted. Plaintiff's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff Merced Valdamar is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Merced Valdamar, Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And/ Or Plaintiff'S Counsel
Off Calendar Jan-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Defendant JOE GROSSMAN's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Merced Valdamar, Production Of Documents is granted. Plaintiff's failure to appear for deposition was a misuse of the discovery process. Plaintiff Merced Valdamar is ordered to appear for her deposition, to be noticed by Defendant and to be completed no later than January 22, 2010. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $340, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- ATTY. JAMES MILLAR, TEL. NO. 981-8100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JEFFREY V. TA OF THE BLEDSOE LAW FIRM TEL. NO. 981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JOE GROSSMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 3 AND 5 BECAUSE THEY ARE NOT BASED ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 BECAUSE ALTHOUGH BASED IN PART ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF RETALIATORY MOTIVE. GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE IT IS BASED AT LEAST IN PART ON PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF A PRIMA FACIE CASE. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/AY
